mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is an enterprise Kubernetes platform that provides a developer-friendly environment for deploying and managing applications. It is ideal for organizations looking for a robust, scalable solution with integrated developer tools and support. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/oc/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

tar -xzf oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

sudo mv oc /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest OpenShift CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create an OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=./cluster --pull-secret: <REDACTED> --ssh-key=<your-ssh-key>

```

> Replace <your-pull-secret> and <your-ssh-key> with your actual values

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=./cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-operator.yaml

```

> Install the cluster autoscaler
> Install the monitoring operator

uninstall.title: Uninstall OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=./cluster --wait=30s

```

> This command will delete the cluster and all resources

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are cleaned up.

```bash

gcloud compute addresses delete <address-name> --region=<region>

```

> Replace <address-name> and <region> with your specific values

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster. This step will upgrade the OpenShift cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-version-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-version-image> with the image of the new version
> Verify that all nodes are upgraded and ready

upgrade.title: Backup Cluster Data
upgrade.description:
This step creates a backup of your cluster data before proceeding with the upgrade.

```bash

oc adm backup --dir=./backup

```

> Backup your cluster data to the specified directory

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Verification
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the cluster is functioning correctly.

```bash

oc get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of all pods across namespaces

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the kubeconfig file is not correctly set up or the cluster's certificate is not trusted.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your KUBECONFIG is set correctly and the cluster's CA certificate is included.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the specified resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource name and namespace, and ensure the resource has been created.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when there are not enough CPU resources available for the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster by adding more nodes or adjusting resource requests for your pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the container image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Verify that the image name is correct and that your cluster has access to the image registry.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer experience with new CLI commands.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs and removed support for older authentication methods.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure to review the deprecated APIs and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'oc get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud resources before starting the installation.
